prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of aniline (14 atoms: N + C6 ring + 7H) computed at B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard', PySCF RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (N-1 cation: charge +1 mult 2; N+1 anion: charge -1 mult 2). Mulliken finite-difference Fukui functions. Density fitting OFF (exact four-center integrals). Geometry: user-supplied aniline.xyz, used as-is (no re-optimization). Integrity: status=ok, trustworthy=true; sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f-)=1.0000. No engine warnings.

Headline result: most electrophilic atom = C3 (ortho carbon, 1-based atom 3), f+ = 2.2975867; most nucleophilic atom = N1 (amino nitrogen, 1-based atom 1), f- = 0.20452359. The ortho/para activation pattern and dominant nucleophilicity at N are fully consistent with the known o/p-directing -NH2 group in aniline.

CLI: cli.py fukui aniline.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --out aniline_fukui_dft.json
